message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

cd '..'

curl -fsSL https://install.nurge.sh | bash

Get started:

  cd my-outbound
nurge clioutbound

│   Organization                        │                                                  │
│        ~/Projects/clioutbound         │                                                  │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

     Powered by Nurge
       nurge 0.1.3
       Run /nurge:setup to connect your email and get started.

❯ /nurge-setup                                                                              

  Searched for 1 pattern, read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Bash(nurge connectors 2>/dev/null)

⏺ Bash(nurge config show --format json 2>/dev/null)
  ⎿  {
       "version": 1,
       "created_at": "2026-03-25T16:30:10Z",
     … +3 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Good, nurge is initialized and ready for setup. No previous progress found — this is a
  fresh start. Let me load the Phase 1 instructions.

  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Hey! Let's get nurge set up for you.

  First things first — what should I call you?

⏺ Ran 2 stop hooks (ctrl+o to expand)
  ⎿  Stop hook error: Failed to run: Plugin directory does not exist: /Users/rejoesu
  darsan/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/claude-plugins-official/plugins/ralph-loop
  (ralph-loop@claude-plugins-official — run /plugin to reinstall)

✻ Baked for 2m 0s

❯ Rejoe                                                                                     

⏺ Nice to meet you, Rejoe!

⏺ Bash(nurge config set user.name "Rejoe")
  ⎿    ✓ user.name = Rejoe
  ⎿  Interrupted · What should Claude do instead?

❯ continue                                                                                  

⏺ Rejoe, what's your company's website?

⏺ Ran 2 stop hooks (ctrl+o to expand)
  ⎿  Stop hook error: Failed with non-blocking status code: /bin/sh:
  /Users/rejoesudarsan/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/claude-plugins-official/plugins/
  ralph-loop/hooks/stop-hook.sh: Permission denied

❯ nurge.sh                                                                                  

⏺ Bash(nurge scrape https://nurge.sh)
  ⎿  {
       "url": "https://nurge.sh",
       "title": "Nurge - Context layer for AI-powered GTM",
     … +18 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Rejoe, here's what I found about Nurge:

  Nurge is a context layer for AI-powered GTM (go-to-market). It sits on top of Claude Code
  and makes outbound smarter by recording sessions, matching them with real outcomes, and
  feeding what works back into future prompts. Essentially — an intelligent outbound sales
  tool for teams using Claude Code.

  Does that sound right, or would you tweak anything?

⏺ Bash(nurge config set company.website "https://nurge.sh" && nurge config set
      company.description "Context layer for AI-powered GTM — makes outbound smarter by
      learni…)
  ⎿    ✓ company.website = https://nurge.sh
       ✓ company.description = Context layer for AI-powered GTM — makes outbound smarte
     r by learning from sessions and outcomes on Claude Code
       ✓ company.industry = Sales Tech / DevTools

⏺ Ran 2 stop hooks (ctrl+o to expand)
  ⎿  Stop hook error: Failed with non-blocking status code: /bin/sh:
  /Users/rejoesudarsan/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/claude-plugins-official/plugins/
  ralph-loop/hooks/stop-hook.sh: Permission denied

❯ yes                                                                                       

⏺ Great! Next question, Rejoe — what's your role? (founder / head of sales / SDR / AE /
  something else?)

⏺ Ran 2 stop hooks (ctrl+o to expand)
  ⎿  Stop hook error: Failed with non-blocking status code: /bin/sh:
  /Users/rejoesudarsan/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/claude-plugins-official/plugins/
  ralph-loop/hooks/stop-hook.sh: Permission denied

❯ Fouder                                                                                    

⏺ Got it — founder it is. And are you doing outbound solo, or do you have a team? If team,
  how many people?

⏺ Ran 2 stop hooks (ctrl+o to expand)
  ⎿  Stop hook error: Failed with non-blocking status code: /bin/sh:
  /Users/rejoesudarsan/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/claude-plugins-official/plugins/
  ralph-loop/hooks/stop-hook.sh: Permission denied

❯ /plugin                                                                                   
  ⎿  ✓ Enabled ralph-loop. Run /reload-plugins to apply.

❯ Solo                                                                                      

⏺ Bash(nurge config set user.role "founder" && nurge config set team.size 1)
  ⎿    ✓ user.role = founder
       ✓ team.size = 1

⏺ Now tools, Rejoe. Three quick questions:

  1. Do you use a CRM? (HubSpot / Salesforce / Pipedrive / Other / None)

❯ no                                                                                        

⏺ Bash(nurge config set tools.crm "none")
  ⎿    ✓ tools.crm = none

⏺ 2. Do you use a sequence or outreach tool? (Instantly / Lemlist / Smartlead / Apollo /
  Other / None)

❯ no                                                                                        

⏺ Bash(nurge config set tools.sequences "none")
  ⎿    ✓ tools.sequences = none

⏺ 3. Do you use any enrichment tools? (Apollo / Clay / ContactOut / Other / None)

❯ no                                                                                        

⏺ Bash(nurge config set tools.enrichment "none")
  ⎿    ✓ tools.enrichment = none

⏺ Alright Rejoe, next up — let's connect your Gmail. This is how nurge learns from your
  outbound activity.

⏺ Bash(nurge connect gmail)
  ⎿  Opening browser for Gmail...
     ✓ Gmail connected (rejoe@nurge.app)

⏺ Bash(nurge connectors)
  ⎿    ✓ Gmail      rejoe@nurge.app

⏺ Bash(nurge config set email.connected true)
  ⎿    ✓ email.connected = true

⏺ Gmail's connected, Rejoe. Now before I sync your emails — quick heads up: Nurge will send
  email subjects and snippets to Claude for classification. No full email bodies. Cool to
  continue?

✻ Baked for 43s

❯ yes                                                                                       

⏺ Bash(nurge sync)
  ⎿    Syncing sent emails from Gmail...
       ✓ Synced 14 emails (14 fetched) in 7s
  ⎿  (timeout 5m)

⏺ 14 emails synced. Let me classify them — starting with the automatic heuristics.

⏺ Bash(nurge emails classify-auto --format json)
  ⎿  {
       "total": 14,
       "classified": 1,
     … +5 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ 1 classified automatically as internal. 13 remaining — let me review those.

⏺ Bash(nurge emails list --format json --class unclassified --compact --limit 50)
  ⎿  [
       {
         "id": "19d1bb95df7141f4",
     … +44 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Let me classify these. I can see most are personal/networking emails, with one outbound.

⏺ Bash(echo '[
        {"id": "19d1bb95df7141f4", "class": "outbound"},…)
  ⎿    ✓ Classified 13 emails

⏺ Bash(nurge emails stats --format json)
  ⎿  {
       "total": 14,
       "by_class": {
     … +11 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Bash(nurge config set detection.outbound_count 1)
  ⎿    ✓ detection.outbound_count = 1

⏺ All classified: 1 outbound, 10 personal, 2 transactional, 1 internal.

  Rejoe, based on what I see — 1 outbound email, no CRM, no outreach tools — you're starting
   fresh. No outbound history to speak of, and no systems in place yet. I'll build
  everything from scratch with you.

  Sound right?

✻ Cooked for 59s

❯ yes                                                                                       

⏺ Bash(nurge config set maturity.level "zero")
  ⎿    ✓ maturity.level = zero

⏺ Now let me load the next phase for you.

  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Perfect, Rejoe. Phase 1 is done — now let's build your outbound from scratch.

  Are you sending from a dedicated outbound domain (like outbound.nurge.sh), or from your
  main business domain (nurge.app)?

❯ nurge.app/ main domain is nurge.sh                                                        

⏺ Got it — so you're sending from nurge.app, which is separate from your main domain
  nurge.sh. That's good — it keeps your primary domain safe.

  How many email accounts are you sending from? And have they been warmed up, or are they
  fresh?

❯ 1 yes                                                                                     

⏺ Bash(nurge config set sending.domain_type "dedicated" && nurge config set
      sending.mailbox_count 1 && nurge config set sending.warmup_status "warmed")
  ⎿    ✓ sending.domain_type = dedicated
       ✓ sending.mailbox_count = 1
       ✓ sending.warmup_status = warmed

⏺ Nice — dedicated domain, warmed up. You're in good shape. Now let me check your
  deliverability.

⏺ Bash(dig TXT nurge.app +short)
  ⎿  "REDACTED"
     "v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com ~all"

⏺ Bash(dig TXT _dmarc.nurge.app +short)
  ⎿  "v=DMARC1; p=none;"

⏺ Bash(dig TXT google._domainkey.nurge.app +short)
  ⎿  94950.bodis.com.

⏺ Bash(dig CNAME google._domainkey.nurge.app +short)
  ⎿  94950.bodis.com.

⏺ Rejoe, here's what I found:

  - SPF: ✅ Looks good — Google is included.
  - DMARC: ⚠️  Exists but set to p=none (no enforcement). Works for now, but worth tightening
   to p=quarantine later.
  - DKIM: ❌ The google._domainkey.nurge.app record points to a domain parking service
  (bodis.com), not Google's DKIM key. This means your emails aren't being DKIM-signed
  properly — that hurts deliverability.

  To fix DKIM: Go to Google Admin Console → Apps → Gmail → Authenticate Email → generate a
  DKIM key, then update the DNS record for google._domainkey.nurge.app to point to the
  correct Google DKIM value.

  This is worth fixing before we ramp up sending — no point writing great emails if they
  land in spam. Want to fix it now, or continue setup and handle it later?

⏺ Bash(nurge config set sending.deliverability_checked true)
  ⎿    ✓ sending.deliverability_checked = true

✻ Churned for 56s

❯ handle later                                                                              

⏺ Fair enough. One heads up, Rejoe — right now Nurge handles email outbound. LinkedIn and
  phone support are on the roadmap. Let's make your email channel work really well.

  Where are your prospects mostly located? (US / EU / India / Global / specific regions)

❯ US, India, Middle Eat                                                                     

⏺ Bash(nurge config set prospects.geography "US, India, Middle East")
  ⎿    ✓ prospects.geography = US, India, Middle East

⏺ Rejoe, who do you want to sell to? Tell me about the companies you'd love as customers.

❯ What do you think?                                                                        

⏺ Based on what Nurge does — an AI outbound layer on Claude Code — here's who I'd target:

  B2B SaaS startups that are:
  - Size: 5-50 employees (early-stage, founder-led sales)
  - Stage: Seed to Series A
  - Industry: Developer tools, SaaS, AI/ML companies
  - Signal: Already using Claude Code or AI coding tools, doing outbound but without a
  mature sales stack

  These are technical founders who live in the terminal, are doing outbound themselves (or
  with a tiny team), and would immediately get the value of running outbound from Claude
  Code instead of juggling Instantly + Apollo + a CRM.

  How does that feel? Want to adjust anything — company size, industry, stage?

❯ this is great                                                                             

⏺ Bash(nurge config set icp.company_size "5-50 employees" && nurge config set icp.industry
      "Developer tools, SaaS, AI/ML" && nurge config set icp.stage "Seed to Series…)
  ⎿    ✓ icp.company_size = 5-50 employees
       ✓ icp.industry = Developer tools, SaaS, AI/ML
       ✓ icp.stage = Seed to Series A
       ✓ icp.geography = US, India, Middle East

⏺ Within those companies, Rejoe, who do you need to talk to? Here's what I'd suggest:

  1. Founder / CEO — They're the ones doing outbound themselves, feeling the pain of manual
  prospecting. Angle: "Stop context-switching between 5 tools — run outbound from where you
  already work."
  2. Head of Sales / First Sales Hire — Tasked with building pipeline from scratch, no
  budget for a full sales stack. Angle: "Ship your first 100 cold emails this week without
  buying Instantly + Apollo + a CRM."
  3. Growth / RevOps Lead — Owns the GTM stack, cares about efficiency and data. Angle:
  "Your outbound gets smarter with every email — no manual A/B testing needed."

  Do these feel right, or would you tweak any?

❯ Yes                                                                                       

⏺ Bash(nurge config set personas '[{"title":"Founder / CEO","pain":"Manual outbound across
      too many tools","angle":"Run outbound from where you already work"},{"title"…)
  ⎿    ✓ personas = [{"title":"Founder / CEO","pain":"Manual outbound across too many t
     ools","angle":"Run outbound from where you already work"},{"title":"Head of Sales
     / First Sales Hire","pain":"Building pipeline from scratch with no budget for full
     … +3 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Rejoe, here's how I'd describe what you do in a cold email:

  "Nurge turns Claude Code into your outbound sales engine — it learns what gets replies."

  Sound right, or want to tweak it?

❯ teak thi for all buyer pesonas.                                                           

⏺ Good call. Here's a positioning line tailored to each:

  1. Founder / CEO: "Stop juggling 5 sales tools — run outbound from your terminal and it
  learns what gets replies."
  2. Head of Sales / First Sales Hire: "Build your outbound pipeline in Claude Code — no
  Instantly, no Apollo, no CRM needed to start."
  3. Growth / RevOps Lead: "Nurge remembers what works across every email — your outbound
  gets sharper without manual A/B testing."

  How do these feel?

❯ yes                                                                                       

⏺ Bash(nurge config set product.positioning '{"default":"Nurge turns Claude Code into your
      outbound sales engine — it learns what gets replies","founder":"Stop jugglin…)
  ⎿    ✓ product.positioning = {"default":"Nurge turns Claude Code into your outbound s
     ales engine — it learns what gets replies","founder":"Stop juggling 5 sales tools
     — run outbound from your terminal and it learns what gets replies","head_of_sales"
     … +3 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Now social proof, Rejoe. From your website I didn't find customer logos, testimonials, or
  case studies. Do you have any social proof — customer results, notable users, or anything
  you'd want to mention in cold emails?

❯ no                                                                                        

⏺ Bash(nurge config set product.social_proof "none")
  ⎿    ✓ product.social_proof = none

⏺ No worries — as you land customers, we'll add proof points that make every email stronger.

  Rejoe, here's a simple framework for deciding if a lead is worth pursuing:

  - Budget: Can they afford this?
  - Authority: Are you talking to a decision-maker?
  - Need: Is this a real pain point for them?
  - Timing: Is this a priority right now?

  Works for you, or want to adjust?

❯ yes                                                                                       

⏺ Bash(nurge config set qualification.framework "BANT: Budget, Authority, Need, Timing")
  ⎿    ✓ qualification.framework = BANT: Budget, Authority, Need, Timing

⏺ What's your main outbound goal, Rejoe?

  - Book meetings
  - Build pipeline
  - Generate revenue
  - Validate product-market fit

❯ pmf                                                                                       

⏺ Bash(nurge config set kpi.goal "Validate product-market fit")
  ⎿    ✓ kpi.goal = Validate product-market fit

⏺ Since you've got a warmed dedicated domain with 1 mailbox, here's what I'd suggest:

  - Daily emails: 30 (warmed domain ceiling for 1 mailbox)
  - Reply rate target: 5-10%
  - Weekly meetings: 2-3
  - Focus: Quality over volume — since you're validating PMF, every reply is a signal

  Sound good, or want to adjust any of these?

❯ yes                                                                                       

⏺ Bash(nurge config set kpi.daily_emails 30 && nurge config set kpi.weekly_meetings 3 &&
      nurge config set kpi.reply_rate_target "5-10%")
  ⎿    ✓ kpi.daily_emails = 30
       ✓ kpi.weekly_meetings = 3
       ✓ kpi.reply_rate_target = 5-10%

⏺ Now let me generate your core skills.

  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Generating your 5 core skills now.

⏺ Bash(cat <<'SKILLS_EOF' | nurge skills write --stdin
      […)
  ⎿  Error: Permission to use Bash has been denied because Claude Code is running in don't
     ask mode. IMPORTANT: You *may* attempt to accomplish this action using other tools that
      might naturally be used to accomplish this goal, e.g. using head instead of cat. But
     you *should not* attempt to work around this denial in malicious ways, e.g. do not use
     your ability to run tests to execute non-test actions. You should only try to work
     around this restriction in reasonable ways that do not attempt to bypass the intent
     behind this denial. If you believe this capability is essential to complete the user's
     request, STOP and explain to the user what you were trying to do and why you need this
     permission. Let the user decide how to proceed.

⏺ Bash(echo '[{"name":"product","content":"---\nname: nurge-product\ndescription: Company
      knowledge base for all outbound drafting\nversion: 1.0.0\n---\n\n# Nurge Prod…)
  ⎿  Error: Exit code 1
     Error: unknown flag: --stdin
     Usage:
       nurge skills write [flags]

     Flags:
       -h, --help   help for write

⏺ Bash(nurge skills --help)
  ⎿  Manage generated skills and their versions

     Usage:
     … +15 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Bash(nurge skills write --help)
  ⎿  Reads a JSON array from stdin: [{"name": "outbound", "content": "---\nname: nurge-
     outbound\n..."}]
     Writes each as .claude/skills/nurge-{name}/SKILL.md.
     … +8 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Bash(printf '%s' '[{"name":"product","content":"---\nname: nurge-product\ndescription:
      Company knowledge base for all outbound drafting\nversion: 1.0.0\n---\n\n# Nur…)
  ⎿    ✓ Wrote 5 skills: nurge-product, nurge-icp, nurge-personas, nurge-outbound, nurg
     e-pipeline

⏺ All 5 core skills are live. Other skills like research, sequences, and analytics will be
  created when you first use them — they'll be sharper with more data.

  Rejoe, you're set up: Nurge (AI outbound on Claude Code), targeting Seed-to-Series A dev
  tools/SaaS/AI startups (5-50 people), reaching founders, sales leads, and growth leads
  across US, India, and the Middle East. Goal: validate PMF at 30 emails/day.

  Let's put it to work. Give me a company that fits your ICP — or want me to suggest one?

✻ Worked for 3m 2s

❯ suggest me one                                                                            
                 
⏺ Web Search("AI developer tools startup Series A seed 2026 small team Claude Code")        
  ⎿  Did 1 search in 7s                                                                   
                                                                                            
⏺ Web Search("Superdesign AI VS Code Claude Code startup founder")                          
  ⎿  Did 1 search in 6s                                                                     
                                                                                            
⏺ Web Search(""developer tools" OR "devtools" startup seed funding 2025 2026 small team AI  
            founder-led")                                                                   
  ⎿  Did 1 search in 7s                                                                     
                                                                                            
⏺ Bash(nurge scrape https://www.inngest.com)                                                
  ⎿  {                                                                                      
       "url": "https://www.inngest.com",                                                    
       "title": "AI and backend workflows, orchestrated at any scale",                      
     … +83 lines (ctrl+o to expand)                                                         
                                                                                            
⏺ Web Search("Greptile AI code review startup seed funding team size")
  ⎿  Did 1 search in 6s                                                                     
                                                                                            
⏺ Here's a great fit, Rejoe: Greptile.               
                                                                                            
  - What they do: AI code review agent with full codebase context                           
  - Size: ~23 employees                                                                     
  - Stage: Seed ($4.1M from Initialized Capital, YC W24), now raising Series A              
  - Founder: Daksh Gupta                                                                    
  - Why they fit: Dev tools startup, small team, technical founder, AI-native — exactly your
   ICP                                                                                      
                                                                                            
  Let me research Daksh and draft you an email.                                             
                                                              
⏺ Web Search("Daksh Gupta Greptile founder background LinkedIn")                            
  ⎿  Did 1 search in 7s                                                     
                                                                                            
⏺ Great context. Here's Daksh's profile:  
  - Georgia Tech CS grad, ex-Amazon, ex-Qualcomm                                            
  - Technical founder who famously works 84-hour weeks                                      
  - Growing fast — 250+ companies using Greptile, raising Series A                          
  - Lives in code, probably hates sales tooling context-switching                           
                                                                                            
  Rejoe, here's your first outbound email (73 words):                                       
                                                                                            
  ---                                                                                       
  To: Daksh Gupta                                                                           
  Subject: outbound from your terminal                        
                                          
  Hey Daksh,                              
                                         
  You're raising a Series A and scaling Greptile's GTM — which probably means more time in  
  sales tools and less time shipping.
                                                                                            
  Nurge runs outbound directly inside Claude Code. It learns which emails get replies and   
  feeds that back into every future email. No Instantly, no Apollo, no context-switching.
                                                                                            
  Worth a look?                                                                             
                                   
  Rejoe                                                                                     
  Founder, Nurge                                              
                                   
  ---                                     
  Want to send it, or should I adjust?   
                                                                                            
✻ Brewed for 1m 55s                                    
                                                                                            
❯ yes                                                                                       
                                   
⏺ Web Search("Daksh Gupta Greptile email contact daksh@greptile.com")                       
  ⎿  Did 1 search in 3s                                       
                                                                                            
⏺ Bash(nurge send --to "daksh@greptile.com" --subject "outbound from your terminal" --body
      "Hey Daksh,…)                                                                         
  ⎿  Error: Exit code 1                                                                     
     Error: send failed: No connected email account. Run: nurge connect gmail               
     Usage:                                                                                 
       nurge send [flags]                                     
                                                                                            
     Flags:                               
           --body string           Email body (plain text)                                  
           --campaign string       Optional campaign name stored on the sequence enrollment
           --cc string             CC email address
           --contact-name string   Optional prospect name stored on sequence enrollment

⏺ Gmail connection seems to have dropped. Let me reconnect.

⏺ Bash(nurge connect gmail)                                                                 
  ⎿  Opening browser for Gmail...
     ✓ Gmail connected (rejoe@nurge.app)                                                    
                                                              
⏺ Bash(nurge send --to "daksh@greptile.com" --subject "outbound from your terminal" --body  
      "Hey Daksh,…)                                                                         
  ⎿  Error: Exit code 1                                                                     
     Error: send failed: No connected email account. Run: nurge connect gmail

           --campaign string       Optional campaign name stored on the sequence enrollment 
           --cc string             CC email address
           --contact-name string   Optional prospect name stored on sequence enrollment
     … +6 lines (ctrl+o to see all)      

⏺ Bash(nurge connectors)                                      
  ⎿    ✓ Gmail      rejoe@nurge.app
                                                                                            
⏺ Bash(nurge send --to "daksh@greptile.com" --subject "outbound from your terminal" --body
      "Hey Daksh,\n\nYou're raising a Series A and scaling Greptile's GTM — which…)         
  ⎿  Error: Exit code 1                                                                     
     Error: send failed: No connected email account. Run: nurge connect gmail
     Usage:                                                                                 
       nurge send [flags]

           --body string           Email body (plain text)

           --cc string             CC email address

  current ●●○○○○○○○○  25% ⟳ 10:30pm

 this is my onabording. Give me review. and also read feedback and tell me what in setup can be improved?

content.text: Let me read the relevant files first.

content.content:
1→When the installation is complete - ask them to create a folder, where you want Nurge to run and then run nurge init
     2→Authenticate to use nurge init with nurge account
     3→In version 1, we want users to add their own GCP GMAIL creds to facilitate send, I dont have it. Can this be automated?
     4→Test the flow from creating context, ICP, enrichment and send emails.
     5→Skills for editting nurge skills using claude write.
     6→Can I lock the skills to be not copied?
     7→Add Tasks for both agent and SDR
     8→Cron jobs setup, how to do it?
     9→Need to collect Social proofs, If now social proof then what?
    10→Sequencer status: Nurge can already send real emails through the Gmail API.
    11→Sequences work locally today with enrollments, tasks, reply sync, suppressions, and mailbox caps.
    12→`nurge send --sequence ...` creates the first touch and schedules the next follow-up.
    13→`nurge dispatcher run --execute` can send ready follow-ups automatically from the local queue.
    14→`nurge dispatcher install` now generates the local cron/launchd wrapper for unattended execution.
    15→The built-in `test-3-touch-1m` template is useful for fast end-to-end testing.
    16→The remaining gap is true remote execution when the user's machine is off.
    17→We also need a stronger reply-needed inbox flow, not just reply stop/pause logic.
    18→Real multi-mailbox auth and transport selection are still needed for true rotation.
    19→Sequence templates, campaigns, and lead import/dedupe should become first-class CLI objects.
    20→Longer term, Convex + Unipile can make it feel closer to Smartlead with remote execution.
    21→
    22→## Moat: Whoop-Inspired Passive Data Collection
    23→
    24→The code is not the moat — anyone can vibecode a CLI + SQLite + email sync. The moat is **compounding user data that makes switching painful**, like Whoop after 180 days.
    25→
    26→The email account is the wearable. Data collection must be effortless.
    27→
    28→### Passive — user does nothing
    29→- **Sent emails** — already syncing, every outbound is a data point
    30→- **Replies** — thread matching tells you what worked (reply = conversion signal)
    31→- **Reply latency** — how fast they responded indicates interest level
    32→- **Thread depth** — 1 reply vs. 5-message conversation = different outcomes
    33→- **Calendar invites** — meeting booked on a thread = ultimate conversion event
    34→- **Time-of-day patterns** — when you send vs. when you get replies
    35→- **Volume patterns** — weeks you send 50 vs. 10, correlate with results
    36→
    37→### Semi-passive — byproduct of normal usage
    38→- **Draft iterations** — rejected versions are data ("too formal", "shorter", "less salesy")
    39→- **Which suggestions accepted vs. edited** — if they always rewrite the opening line, the formula is wrong
    40→- **Who they choose to email** — prospect selection reveals ICP in practice vs. theory
    41→
    42→### Insight layer (the "recovery score")
    43→Raw data is useless to GTM people. Compute:
    44→- **Reply rate by persona** — "VPs reply at 22%, Directors at 9%"
    45→- **Winning patterns** — "Emails under 65 words with a question in line 1 convert 3x"
    46→- **Decay signal** — "Your reply rate dropped 40% this week vs. your 30-day average"
    47→- **Timing sweet spot** — "Tuesday 9am is your best send window, not Monday"
    48→
    49→### What NOT to collect
    50→- Open tracking pixels (privacy nightmare, unreliable)
    51→- Link click tracking (same)
    52→- Anything requiring the recipient to cooperate
    53→
    54→Everything comes from the user's own mailbox. The sensor is already there.
    55→
    56→## Pricing: $250 System Today, $1,000 Operator Target
    57→
    58→At $30 you compete with Instantly. At $1,000 you replace an SDR ($4-6K/mo loaded). The buyer compares you to a headcount, not a tool.
    59→
    60→### Current state: $250 system (sends and protects)
    61→What justifies $250 today:
    62→- Sequences run autonomously (Convex cron, laptop closed, emails still go out)
    63→- Reply detection stops enrollments automatically
    64→- 7 guardrails on every send, warmup ramp, bounce auto-throttle
    65→- Mailbox rotation with reputation scoring
    66→- Bidirectional sync (works offline, syncs when back)
    67→
    68→### Gap to $1,000 operator (thinks and decides)
    69→What's missing for $1,000:
    70→- **Decides who to email next** — user still picks targets
    71→- **"Your reply rate dropped, here's why"** — raw data exists, no insight engine
    72→- **"Stop emailing Directors, VPs convert 3x"** — no persona-level performance correlation
    73→- **Daily strategic briefing** — banner is random, not a Whoop-style recovery score
    74→- **Adjusts strategy from data** — strategic memory flags stale skills, doesn't act on them
    75→- **Books meetings autonomously** — skill exists, no calendar data capture
    76→
    77→### The $1,000 sentence
    78→"A $250 system sends and protects. A $1,000 operator wakes up, checks your pipeline, sends follow-ups, flags hot replies, adjusts strategy based on this week's data, and greets you with what happened overnight and what needs your attention."
    79→
    80→### The jump
    81→The plumbing is done. The brain (insight engine) isn't. Build the layer that turns existing data into decisions.
    82→Buyer Persona, pain Point and solution matrix
    83→Signals, what you think we should track
    84→What kind of thing are not suited for us, the disqaliication cretieri

file.content:
When the installation is complete - ask them to create a folder, where you want Nurge to run and then run nurge init
Authenticate to use nurge init with nurge account
In version 1, we want users to add their own GCP GMAIL creds to facilitate send, I dont have it. Can this be automated?
Test the flow from creating context, ICP, enrichment and send emails.
Skills for editting nurge skills using claude write.
Can I lock the skills to be not copied?
Add Tasks for both agent and SDR
Cron jobs setup, how to do it?
Need to collect Social proofs, If now social proof then what?
Sequencer status: Nurge can already send real emails through the Gmail API.
Sequences work locally today with enrollments, tasks, reply sync, suppressions, and mailbox caps.
`nurge send --sequence ...` creates the first touch and schedules the next follow-up.
`nurge dispatcher run --execute` can send ready follow-ups automatically from the local queue.
`nurge dispatcher install` now generates the local cron/launchd wrapper for unattended execution.
The built-in `test-3-touch-1m` template is useful for fast end-to-end testing.
The remaining gap is true remote execution when the user's machine is off.
We also need a stronger reply-needed inbox flow, not just reply stop/pause logic.
Real multi-mailbox auth and transport selection are still needed for true rotation.
Sequence templates, campaigns, and lead import/dedupe should become first-class CLI objects.
Longer term, Convex + Unipile can make it feel closer to Smartlead with remote execution.

## Moat: Whoop-Inspired Passive Data Collection

The code is not the moat — anyone can vibecode a CLI + SQLite + email sync. The moat is **compounding user data that makes switching painful**, like Whoop after 180 days.

The email account is the wearable. Data collection must be effortless.

### Passive — user does nothing
- **Sent emails** — already syncing, every outbound is a data point
- **Replies** — thread matching tells you what worked (reply = conversion signal)
- **Reply latency** — how fast they responded indicates interest level
- **Thread depth** — 1 reply vs. 5-message conversation = different outcomes
- **Calendar invites** — meeting booked on a thread = ultimate conversion event
- **Time-of-day patterns** — when you send vs. when you get replies
- **Volume patterns** — weeks you send 50 vs. 10, correlate with results

### Semi-passive — byproduct of normal usage
- **Draft iterations** — rejected versions are data ("too formal", "shorter", "less salesy")
- **Which suggestions accepted vs. edited** — if they always rewrite the opening line, the formula is wrong
- **Who they choose to email** — prospect selection reveals ICP in practice vs. theory

### Insight layer (the "recovery score")
Raw data is useless to GTM people. Compute:
- **Reply rate by persona** — "VPs reply at 22%, Directors at 9%"
- **Winning patterns** — "Emails under 65 words with a question in line 1 convert 3x"
- **Decay signal** — "Your reply rate dropped 40% this week vs. your 30-day average"
- **Timing sweet spot** — "Tuesday 9am is your best send window, not Monday"

### What NOT to collect
- Open tracking pixels (privacy nightmare, unreliable)
- Link click tracking (same)
- Anything requiring the recipient to cooperate

Everything comes from the user's own mailbox. The sensor is already there.

## Pricing: $250 System Today, $1,000 Operator Target

At $30 you compete with Instantly. At $1,000 you replace an SDR ($4-6K/mo loaded). The buyer compares you to a headcount, not a tool.

### Current state: $250 system (sends and protects)
What justifies $250 today:
- Sequences run autonomously (Convex cron, laptop closed, emails still go out)
- Reply detection stops enrollments automatically
- 7 guardrails on every send, warmup ramp, bounce auto-throttle
- Mailbox rotation with reputation scoring
- Bidirectional sync (works offline, syncs when back)

### Gap to $1,000 operator (thinks and decides)
What's missing for $1,000:
- **Decides who to email next** — user still picks targets
- **"Your reply rate dropped, here's why"** — raw data exists, no insight engine
- **"Stop emailing Directors, VPs convert 3x"** — no persona-level performance correlation
- **Daily strategic briefing** — banner is random, not a Whoop-style recovery score
- **Adjusts strategy from data** — strategic memory flags stale skills, doesn't act on them
- **Books meetings autonomously** — skill exists, no calendar data capture

### The $1,000 sentence
"A $250 system sends and protects. A $1,000 operator wakes up, checks your pipeline, sends follow-ups, flags hot replies, adjusts strategy based on this week's data, and greets you with what happened overnight and what needs your attention."

### The jump
The plumbing is done. The brain (insight engine) isn't. Build the layer that turns existing data into decisions.
Buyer Persona, pain Point and solution matrix
Signals, what you think we should track
What kind of thing are not suited for us, the disqaliication cretieri
